summary.narrative: Governor Healey's first term focused on tax cuts, housing affordability, climate initiatives, and federal infrastructure funding. Second-term agenda emphasizes cost reduction, education, and quality of life improvements. Facing reelection challenge with criticism of affordability policies and fiscal management.
summary.grade_rationale: Delivered on major initiatives (school meals, community college, tax package, federal funding) but faces criticism on affordability outcomes. Housing and climate promises partially implemented or ongoing.

promises.normalized: Electrify all MBTA and school buses by 2030; full public transit by 2040
Implement Affordable Homes Act to address housing costs
Permanent free school meals and free community college for 25+
Secure $9B federal infrastructure funding for transportation projects

data_gaps: No detailed breakdown of tax cut implementation details or fiscal impact
Limited information on ICE restriction executive order outcomes
No specific metrics on life sciences job creation follow-up results
Missing timelines and current status of Affordable Homes Act passage
Lack of data on climate initiative progress since 2023 announcement
No information on electoral margin or campaign finance details
Limited detail on early literacy plan implementation status
No cost estimates for major initiatives or budget trade-offs documented
